context.projectOverview: agent-bober telegram adapter — FINAL sprint (7 of 7). Sprints 1-6 DONE. The adapter has: sendSafe + sendSafeKeyboard + sendSafeForEdit/sendSafeEdit funnel chokepoints (outbound.ts); BotTransport (grammy in bot.ts only); router + handlers/{capture,prioritize,approvals,upload}.ts; streaming.ts (in-place edits) + digest.ts (silent). Sprint 7 adds a READ-ONLY /fleet secretary view: a PURE renderFleetView(bundle) groups .bober/fleet-synthesis.json findings by FactRecord.subject into one section per agent (label + one-line summary + round + confidence + count, header shows rounds); shared by BOTH a /fleet command AND the Sprint 6 streaming path. type-only imports of SynthesisBundle/FactRecord (NO runtime coupling to src/fleet or better-sqlite3). No run/fleet/scheduler logic; no npm dep.

completedSprints.title: telegram adapter Sprints 1-6
completedSprints.summary: transport+whitelist+sendSafe; router+capture; prioritize; approve gate (keyboard.ts/approvals.ts); upload opt-in + funnel unification (sendSafeKeyboard); streaming.ts (1 send + N in-place edits) + digest.ts (silent). grammy isolated to bot.ts. Reuse: whitelist isAllowed/denialReply (feat-2 denial id-echo), sendSafe funnel, streaming.ts renderer wiring.

relevantFiles: src/fleet/synthesis.ts (SynthesisBundle { rounds, childResults, findings } — collect() write path of .bober/fleet-synthesis.json; curator pins exact shape + write path)
src/telegram/streaming.ts (wire renderFleetView into the streaming path)
src/telegram/handlers/prioritize.ts (injected-reader + whitelist handler pattern to mirror)

contract.title: Multi-LLM "secretary" fleet view (/fleet command + per-agent streaming sections)
contract.description: Add a read-only secretary view that surfaces what each LLM/agent produced in the most recent fleet run. A pure renderer reads the head-written .bober/fleet-synthesis.json (SynthesisBundle { rounds, childResults, findings } from src/fleet/synthesis.ts:29), groups findings by FactRecord.subject (the per-agent childFolder, src/fleet/shared-blackboard.ts:74-105), and emits one section per agent showing the agent label, a one-line summary, round, confidence, and that agent's finding count. One shared renderer feeds two surfaces: an on-demand /fleet command and the Sprint 6 streaming/digest path. All output goes through the sendSafe funnel; raw findings[].value is summarized to one line, never sent verbatim. No run/fleet/scheduler logic is added — this stays a thin read+render adapter and adds no npm dependency (SynthesisBundle/FactRecord are type-only imports).

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` (tsc) compiles the fleet-view additions with zero TypeScript errors; SynthesisBundle and FactRecord are imported as type-only (no runtime import of src/fleet or better-sqlite3 into src/telegram).

A unit test passes the renderer a SynthesisBundle whose findings carry >=2 distinct subjects and asserts the output has exactly one section per distinct subject, each containing that agent's label, a one-line summary, the round, the confidence, and that subject's finding count.

A unit test shows that when fleet-synthesis.json is absent or has zero findings (injected reader returns null / { findings: [] }), the /fleet handler returns a non-error 'no recent fleet run' message and throws nothing.
A unit test asserts the rendered header includes the run's round count (SynthesisBundle.rounds).

A unit test with a transport spy and an over-long finding value asserts /fleet and the streamed fleet sections route through sendSafe and that the raw value is truncated/summarized to one line — the verbatim payload never reaches the transport.
A unit test shows a non-whitelisted sender's /fleet is denied with the numeric-id echo (per feat-2) and performs no synthesis-file read (verified via an injected reader spy that is never called).

Running a fleet with a blackboard, then sending /fleet in Telegram, shows one labeled section per agent with summary/round/confidence/count; a live fleet run streams those per-agent sections by editing one message in place (reusing Sprint 6 streaming).

nonGoals: Do not add run, fleet, or scheduler execution logic — read the existing .bober/fleet-synthesis.json artifact only; this stays a presentation adapter.
Do not couple src/telegram to better-sqlite3 or import SharedBlackboard at runtime — read the JSON artifact and import SynthesisBundle/FactRecord as type-only.
Do not emit raw findings[].value verbatim — summarize/truncate to one line and route through sendSafe.
Do not implement Tier 2 (per-LLM bot identities / Bot API 10.0 bot-to-bot) or Tier 3 (Secretary Mode) — both are deferred to sibling specs per the enriched spec outOfScope.
Do not add an npm dependency — feat-9 introduces none.
Do not bypass the whitelist or the sendSafe funnel.
stopConditions: `npm run build` exits zero and the renderer/grouping, empty-state, header-rounds, sendSafe-truncation, and whitelist-denial unit tests all pass.
The per-agent grouping is by FactRecord.subject and each section carries label + one-line summary + round + confidence + count.
Both the /fleet command and the streaming path emit through sendSafe; no raw payload is sent verbatim.
contract.definitionOfDone: A whitelisted user can run /fleet and receive one labeled section per agent from the most recent fleet run (label + one-line summary + round + confidence + finding count, with a header showing the round count), read from .bober/fleet-synthesis.json and grouped by FactRecord.subject. The same renderer feeds the Sprint 6 streaming/digest path so a live run streams per-agent sections in place. Missing/empty synthesis yields a graceful 'no recent fleet run' reply. All output passes through sendSafe with raw payloads truncated to one line; non-whitelisted /fleet is denied with an id-echo and reads nothing. No run/fleet/scheduler logic and no npm dependency are added.
assumptions: The head writes the SynthesisBundle (src/fleet/synthesis.ts:29-39 collect()) to .bober/fleet-synthesis.json after a fleet run; the generator should confirm the exact write path and, if it differs, read that path (the shape — { rounds, childResults, findings } — is the contract).
FactRecord (src/state/facts.ts:36) exposes subject (per-agent childFolder), value (payload), and confidence; predicate is 'finding'. Grouping key = subject.
The sendSafe funnel (Sprint 1) and the in-place streaming sender (Sprint 6, src/telegram/streaming.ts) already exist and are the only outbound path; this sprint feeds them, it does not replace them.
The synthesis file reader is injected (a function returning the parsed bundle or null) so unit tests drive fixtures without touching disk; the production reader uses node:fs/promises.
outOfScope: Live mid-run blackboard reads via `agent-bober blackboard read` (Q7 resolved to the JSON artifact only).
Rendering childResults (PortfolioReport) detail beyond what the per-agent finding sections need.
Any change to how the fleet writes fleet-synthesis.json.
contract.generatorNotes: Add src/telegram/fleet-view.ts exporting (a) a PURE renderFleetView(bundle: SynthesisBundle): string[] (or section[]) that groups bundle.findings by FactRecord.subject, and per group builds a section = agent label (subject) + one-line summary of the latest finding (truncate value to ~1 line) + round + confidence + count; header line includes bundle.rounds; and (b) the /fleet command handler that loads the bundle via an injected reader (default: read .bober/fleet-synthesis.json with node:fs/promises, JSON.parse, return null on ENOENT/parse-failure), gates on the whitelist (reuse feat-2), and emits via sendSafe. Import `type { SynthesisBundle }` from ../fleet/synthesis.js and `type { FactRecord }` from ../state/facts.js (TYPE-ONLY — no runtime coupling). Register /fleet in the bot router (src/telegram/bot.ts) alongside the other slash commands. Wire the Sprint 6 streaming path (src/telegram/streaming.ts / feat-8) to call renderFleetView so a live run streams the same per-agent sections via in-place edits. Empty/missing synthesis → a friendly 'no recent fleet run' line, never a throw. ESM .js imports, `import type`, no `any`, node:fs/promises only.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Drive renderFleetView with a fixture bundle whose findings have >=2 distinct subjects and assert one section per subject with label+summary+round+confidence+count, and that the header shows rounds. Inject a reader returning null and assert the /fleet handler replies 'no recent fleet run' and does not throw. With a transport spy and an over-long finding value, assert output goes through sendSafe and the verbatim payload is truncated (never emitted whole). Assert a non-whitelisted /fleet is denied with the id-echo and the injected reader spy is never called. Confirm via the diff that src/telegram imports SynthesisBundle/FactRecord type-only (no runtime src/fleet or better-sqlite3 import) and that no npm dependency was added (package.json unchanged). Run build + the fleet-view unit tests.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
